title: Methods Should Not Be Empty in Java
description: An empty method is generally considered a bad practice as it can lead to confusion, readability, and maintenance issues. Empty methods bring no functionality and are misleading to others as they might think the method implementation fulfills a specific and identified requirement.
recommendation: There are several reasons for a method not to have a body - it is an unintentional omission, it is not yet or never will be, supported, or the method is an intentionally-blank override. In these cases, an exception should be thrown or a nested comment should explain the reason for the blank override.
impact: Empty methods can lead to confusion in code readability and maintenance. They can also result in unexpected behavior in production if they result from an unintentional omission.
